Removal audit

Two cleanups in one branch. Agentation was unused. Release channels were unused and broken: the docs told users to run an install command against a git branch that has never existed. Every claim below was checked against the repo rather than assumed.

Every surface
ScopeRemoved
docs siteAgentationWrapper + its mount in layout.tsx, both npm packages, lockfiles
plugin agentsrc/agents/ui-feedback.md + its eval spec
lifecycle hookagentation-context.ts, deregistered from both hooks.json and the entries map
skillsmcp-patterns, configure (one file was entirely the agentation section), setup, doctor, verify, chain-patterns
configMCP server blocks in .claude/templates, rows in hand-written docs pages

A hook registered in hooks.json but missing from the entries map is silently dead in this repo, so both had to go together. Deleting the agent also removed its agent-scoped PreToolUse hook, which is why the hook total drops by 2, not 1.

Why this was broken, not merely unused
Docs instruct claude plugin marketplace add orchestkit@betadocumented
beta branch on origindoes not exist
alpha branch on origindoes not exist
channel-sync.yml / prerelease.ymlnever run, not once
release-please prerelease confignone
prerelease tags ever cutone, v7.0.0-rc.1

Anyone following the getting-started page got an error, because the command resolves a git ref that is not there. That made "leave it alone" the one strictly bad option.

Re-stamped across manifests, CLAUDE.md, hooks.json, READMEs
Agents
3736
ui-feedback deleted
Hooks
218216
149 global (was 150) + 45 agent-scoped (was 46) + 22 skill-scoped
Skills
114
unchanged

A test asserted 217 total, assuming a single hook removal. Corrected to 216 against the pre-change file rather than trusting either number.

Checked, not assumed
Full build (plugins/ + generated docs)exit 0
Hook tests7222 passed / 293 files
Docs-site tests318 passed
Count gates (validate-counts)PASS
Live refs to ui-feedback0
Live refs to agentation0

Remaining string matches are CHANGELOG history, a dated analysis note, archived playground HTML, and hook-test comments that document why the counts changed. All deliberately preserved. The one docs-site test failure is the pre-existing web-vitals-reporter flake, which also fails on clean main.

Open risk, stated rather than buried: the skill-eval workflow scores doctor at 42% and setup at 11%. It is not a required check, the stored quality baseline is empty ("no committed results"), and the same workflow failed on two other recent branches, so there is no way to prove from stored data whether these are pre-existing. The failing criteria (hook categories, common hook issues, remediation guidance) do not correspond to what was removed here.
